title: Gentianodes pedicellata
intro: Gentianodes pedicellata, also known as Smallflower Gentian or Little Gentian, is a perennial plant species in the Gentianaceae family, characterized by its small, white or yellowish flowers and narrow, lance-shaped leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Gentianodes pedicellata include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Gentianodes pedicellata is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in herbal infusions and decoctions to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Gentianodes pedicellata is a species within the family Gentianaceae. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and is a flowering plant. This species is classified as Gentianodes pedicellata within the genus Gentianodes. The taxonomy is further supported by its classification as a type of shrub or herb. Additionally, Gentianodes pedicellata is native to certain regions.
uses: Gentianodes pedicellata, also known as the desert gentian, has been traditionally used in medicinal practices by indigenous communities. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever and pain. In some cases, Gentianodes pedicellata has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as colds and coughs. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential benefits. Further studies are necessary to confirm its efficacy in treating various health conditions.

constituents: Gentianodes pedicellata contains various active constituents, including iridoids and triterpenoids, which are known for their potential health benefits. parts: Gentianodes pedicellata, also known as the narrow-leaved gentian, is a plant species used in traditional medicine. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are considered medicinal parts.
